info.title: 1Claw API
info.description:
Secure secret management for AI agents. Provides vaults, secrets,
policy-based access control, agent identity, Intents API,
sharing, billing, and audit logging.

All endpoints require JWT Bearer authentication unless marked with
`security: []`.

servers.description: Shroud TEE Proxy (Intents API + LLM proxy)

tags.name: Authentication
tags.description: Credential exchange, MFA, device auth, token management

tags.name: Treasury
tags.description: Multi-sig treasury wallets (Safe) and agent access requests

tags.name: Treasury Wallets
tags.description: Multi-chain wallet generation for human users (replaces CDP embedded wallets)

tags.name: Approvals
tags.description: Human-in-the-loop approval workflow for agent actions

tags.name: Platform
tags.description: Platform API for developers building on 1Claw (plt_ keys, user provisioning, bootstrap templates)

post.description:
Returns a short-lived EdDSA-signed JWT (`access_token`). Standard claims include `sub`
(`agent:<uuid>`), `org`, `scopes`, `vault_ids`, optional `intents_api_enabled`, optional
`shroud_enabled`, optional `llm_token_billing` / `stripe_customer_id` when org LLM billing is on.

When **`shroud_enabled`** is true, the JWT payload may include **`shroud_config`**: a JSON object
mirroring the agent row in Vault (same shape as `ShroudConfig` on `GET /v1/agents/{id}`).
**Shroud** (TEE proxy) decodes this on each LLM request and runs **PolicyEngine** after the
global inspection pipeline so per-agent limits and threat **block** actions apply without a
separate policy fetch. Re-exchange the agent token after changing `shroud_config` so the JWT
is fresh.

User JWTs from password, API key, or device flow do **not** include `shroud_config`.

post.summary: Exchange user API key for JWT

get.description:
Returns the Ed25519 public key used to sign JWTs.
Use this to verify tokens independently (e.g. in a TEE proxy or
gateway). No authentication required.

For OIDC-compliant relying parties (Anthropic Workload Identity
Federation, etc.) prefer the JWKS endpoint at
`/.well-known/jwks.json` together with the discovery document
at `/.well-known/openid-configuration` — those advertise both
Ed25519 and RS256 keys keyed by `kid` and survive key rotation.

post.summary: Exchange a 1claw token for an OIDC federation token (RFC 8693)
post.description:
Mints a short-lived RS256-signed JWT targeted at an external
relying party (e.g. Anthropic Workload Identity Federation).
The relying party validates the token via this issuer's JWKS
URL (`/.well-known/jwks.json`) and exchanges it for its own
short-lived service credentials.

Requirements:
- The agent that owns the `subject_token` must have
  `federation_enabled = true` and the requested `audience`
  must appear in `federation_audiences`.
- The `subject_token` must be a valid 1claw agent JWT or
  `ocv_` API key.
- Optional `scope` narrows the agent's existing scopes — it
  cannot escalate.

Returns 503 when `ONECLAW_JWT_RS256_SIGNING_KEY_ID` is not
configured on the server.

get.description:
Standard OpenID Connect discovery document advertising the
issuer URL, JWKS URL, supported algorithms (`EdDSA`, `RS256`),
and the RFC 8693 token-exchange endpoint. External IdPs
(Anthropic WIF, Okta, Auth0, etc.) read this URL to learn
where 1claw publishes its JWKS.

Public keys for every active version of every JWT signing
key (Ed25519 + RSA-2048). Each entry includes a `kid` so
consumers can validate tokens issued before the most recent
key rotation. Cached for 5 minutes via `Cache-Control` and
CORS-permissive for browser-based IdP consoles.

post.description:
Always returns the same message whether or not the email exists (no account enumeration).
Only password-based accounts receive mail.

post.summary: Set a new password using reset token from email
Set initial password for platform users
post.description: Only allowed when the user has no password set (platform_oidc users after claiming).
Sends a 6-digit verification code to the new email address. Code expires in 10 minutes.
Returns a WebAuthn challenge for creating a new passkey credential.
Agents can request policy changes or other sensitive actions that require human approval.

post.summary: Confirm MFA enrollment with a TOTP code

201.description: API key created (full key shown once)

post.description:
Returns a full export of the authenticated user's data including
profile, vaults, agents, secrets metadata, and policies. Intended
for GDPR data-portability requests. Only available to human users
(not agents).

Register a new mobile device for the authenticated user. Human-only.
The device public key is used for step-up authentication challenges.

get.description: Returns all registered mobile devices for the authenticated user.

delete.description: Removes a registered device, invalidating its keys and push tokens.

post.description:
Creates a cryptographic challenge bound to a specific action (e.g. approving
a high-risk transaction). The device signs the challenge nonce to prove
possession of the private key.

Submit a signed challenge nonce to complete step-up authentication.
Returns a short-lived step-up token that can be used for the bound action.

Associates a push notification token (APNs or FCM) with a registered device
so the server can send approval requests and alerts.

Enable client-side encryption on a vault. Requires Business or Enterprise plan.
Only the key's SHA-256 fingerprint is stored — the key never touches the server.

delete.description:
Disable client-side encryption. Existing CMEK-encrypted secrets still require
the key to decrypt. New secrets will use HSM-only encryption.

post.description:
Re-encrypts all secrets from the old CMEK key to the new one.
Keys are passed in headers (TLS-only) and exist in server memory
only during the rotation. Batched in groups of 100 secrets.

post.summary: Enable MPC custody on a vault
post.description:
Enable MPC custody on an existing vault. Requires Business or Enterprise plan.
Splits secret encryption keys across multiple providers using the specified
custody mode (e.g. 2-of-2, 2-of-3).

parameters.name: x-client-share
parameters.description: Base64-encoded client key share for MPC 2-of-2 vaults. Required when the vault uses 2-of-2 MPC custody.

get.summary: List all versions of a secret
Retrieve a specific version of a secret

200.description: Decrypted secret value at the specified version

410.description: Version has been disabled or expired

post.description:
Disables a version so it can no longer be read. The version is
retained for audit purposes but returns 410 on read attempts.

Generates a cryptographically random value and stores it as a new
version of the secret. The previous version is preserved in history.
Requires rotate or write permission.

201.description: New version created with server-generated value

post.description:
Public endpoint (no auth required).

**With `human_email`:** Creates a pending enrollment for that account's org,
emails Allow/Deny links, and returns `approval_url` in the JSON body (use if email
is delayed). The API key is NOT returned until the human approves.

**Name only (omit `human_email`):** Creates a link-only pending enrollment.
The response includes `approval_url`; the human opens it while signed in to
approve the agent into their org.

Anti-spam: IP rate limiting, per-email cooldown, caps on pending rows.

201.description: Enrollment processed (uniform response to prevent email enumeration)
Agent created with one-time API key

get.summary: Get the calling agent's own profile

post.description:
Rotates the agent's Ed25519 SSH keypair and P-256 ECDH keypair.
New private keys are stored in the __agent-keys vault; old keys are overwritten.
User-only endpoint — agents cannot rotate their own identity keys.

post.summary: Add a smart account (Safe) for this agent on a chain
post.description:
Use after deploying a Safe on a new chain. Multi-chain; one Safe per chain.
Replaces any existing entry for the same chain_id.

Replay protection: send an optional **Idempotency-Key** header (e.g. UUID or opaque string).
Duplicate requests with the same key within 24 hours return the cached transaction response
(no second sign/broadcast). Omit the header for non-idempotent submissions.

parameters.name: Idempotency-Key
parameters.description: Optional key for replay protection; duplicate requests return cached response.

200.description: Transaction previously created with same Idempotency-Key (replay-safe response)

409.description: Idempotency-Key in use by another in-flight request; retry later.

post.description:
Signs a transaction inside the server (or TEE when using Shroud) but does
**not** broadcast it. The caller receives the raw `signed_tx` hex and
`tx_hash` so it can submit to any RPC of its choosing.

All agent guardrails (allowlists, value caps, daily limits) are enforced
exactly as for the submit endpoint. The signed transaction is recorded for
audit and daily-limit tracking with `status: "sign_only"`.

post.summary: Provision a signing key for a chain

get.summary: List signing keys for an agent

post.summary: Rotate a signing key for a chain

delete.summary: Deactivate a signing key for a chain

post.summary: Export a signing key (private key included)
post.description:
Export the private key for an agent's signing key. Requires re-authentication
via the X-Auth-Confirm header containing the user's account password.
Human users only — agents cannot export keys.

post.summary: Unified signing intent (EIP-191, EIP-712, EIP-2718 types 0-4)

get.summary: Get chain by name or ID

parameters.name: identifier
parameters.description: Chain name (e.g. "ethereum") or numeric chain ID

post.summary: Create a share link for a secret

get.description: Returns the vault id for the caller's org agent-keys vault (used for revealing agent identity keys). Users only; 404 if the vault does not exist.

delete.summary: Remove a member from the organization

post.summary: Start a subscription via Stripe Checkout

get.summary: Get subscription, usage, and credit summary

post.summary: Top up prepaid credits via Stripe

get.description: Returns whether LLM token billing is enabled, optional Stripe billing credit balance (metered scope), and estimated cycle usage from the upcoming invoice—including per-line metered rows when Stripe returns them (amounts; quantities such as tokens when present).

post.description: Creates a Stripe Checkout session for the LLM token billing pricing plan. Returns a checkout URL to redirect the user. After the user completes checkout, a webhook activates LLM billing for the org.
Disables LLM token billing for the org and cancels all active Stripe subscriptions for the LLM pricing plan. Agents will fall back to direct provider routing. You can re-enable it at any time.

delete.summary: Delete a treasury and its signers

post.summary: Add a signer to a treasury

delete.summary: Remove a signer from a treasury

get.summary: List access requests for a treasury

post.summary: Generate multi-chain wallets for the authenticated user
post.description:
Generates keypairs for the requested chains (or all supported chains if omitted).
Private keys are stored in a per-org `__treasury-keys` vault with tier-appropriate
MPC custody. Skips chains where the user already has an active wallet. Requires
Pro or higher billing tier. Human users only — agents get 403.

get.summary: List the authenticated user's treasury wallets
Get the user's active wallet for a specific chain

delete.summary: Deactivate the user's wallet for a specific chain

post.summary: Export the private key for a treasury wallet
post.description:
Returns the raw private key hex for the user's active wallet on
the given chain. Requires re-authentication via the `X-Auth-Confirm`
header (account password). Audit-logged as `treasury_wallet.export`.
Human users only.

post.summary: Rotate the user's wallet key for a chain
post.description:
Generates a new keypair, deactivates the old wallet, and creates a
new active wallet. The old private key version is retained in the
vault for audit. Requires Pro or higher.

put.description:
Manually set an organization's billing tier to free, pro, business, or enterprise.
For testing, manual upgrades, and trial grants — does not create a Stripe subscription.
Setting to "pro", "business", or "enterprise" sets period_end to now + duration_days (default 365).
Setting to "free" clears subscription data. Use duration_days: 90 for a 3-month trial.

post.description:
Deletes every row in `usage_events` for all organizations. Resets monthly
request counts used for free-tier / x402 quota. Does not change prepaid credit
balances or Stripe. **Platform admin only** (same guard as other `/v1/admin/*` routes).

200.description: Usage table cleared; returns number of deleted rows

post.summary: Reset API usage for a user's organization
post.description:
Looks up a registered user by email and deletes all `usage_events` rows for that
user's `org_id`. Resets free-tier / monthly quota for the whole org (not other orgs).
**Platform admin only.**

200.description: Usage cleared for the user's org

get.description: Returns recent Shroud proxy activity for the organization (LLM requests, inspections, policy actions).

post.description: Called by the Shroud proxy to record activity events. Not intended for external use.

get.description: Aggregated threat metrics for the organization from `shroud_activity` (detectors, blocked counts, recent flagged requests). Query `period` selects the window; the previous window of equal length is used for request volume trend.

post.description: Register a new platform app for building on top of 1Claw. Returns an API key (plt_ prefix) that must be saved immediately.

403.description: Only human users can register platform apps

get.description: List all platform apps in the organization.

403.description: Only human users can delete platform apps

post.description: Create a template that defines what vault, agents, and policies to bootstrap for each connected user.

post.summary: Provision or look up a platform user
post.description: Upserts a user using either an OIDC subject_token (verified against the platform app's JWKS) or an email address. Returns the user handle and connection ID.

get.description: List all users connected to this platform app.

post.summary: Bootstrap resources for a connected user
post.description: Executes a template to create vault, agent, and policies for the connected user. Returns a claim URL and token for the user to claim their resources.
Mints a fresh 10-minute claim token for an already-bootstrapped connection without re-provisioning resources. Use when the original claim URL has expired.

get.description: Returns audit events related to this platform app.
Returns platform apps connected to the calling user's account.

delete.description: Disconnect the calling user from a platform app.

get.description:
Verify a claim token and preview what was provisioned (app name, vaults, agents, policies).
Public endpoint — the token itself is the authentication.

parameters.name: token
parameters.description: The `ct_` prefixed claim token from the bootstrap response

post.description:
Redeem a one-time claim token, marking the connection as claimed.
Public endpoint — the token itself is the authentication. Returns 409 if already claimed, 410 if expired.

parameters.name: token
parameters.description: The `ct_` prefixed claim token from the bootstrap response

get.description:
Returns approvals for the authenticated user's organization.
Human-only. Supports filtering by status and pagination.
Returns details for a single approval by ID.

post.description:
Submit a decision (approve or reject) for a pending approval.
Human-only. The approval must be in `pending` status.

ApiKeyAuth.description: 1ck_ prefixed API key used as Bearer token

IncludeSignedTx.name: include_signed_tx
IncludeSignedTx.description: Set to `true` or `1` to include the raw signed transaction hex in the response. Omitted by default to reduce key exfiltration risk. Only the literal values "true" or "1" enable inclusion; any other value or omission returns responses without signed_tx. Applies to GET /v1/agents/{agent_id}/transactions and GET /v1/agents/{agent_id}/transactions/{tx_id}.

agent_id.description: Optional when using key-only auth (ocv_ keys auto-resolve agent)

TokenExchangeRequest.description:
RFC 8693 token-exchange request body. `subject_token_type`
accepts the standard JWT URI or 1claw's API-key URI:
  - `urn:ietf:params:oauth:token-type:jwt`
  - `urn:1claw:params:oauth:token-type:api-key`

subject_token.description: 1claw JWT or `ocv_` API key authorising the exchange.

audience.description: Required `aud` claim for the issued federation token (must be in agent's allowlist).

scope.description: Optional space-separated subset of the agent's existing scopes.

status.description:
Outcome of the reset request:
- email_sent: password reset email dispatched
- no_account: no account found for the email
- social_account: account uses Google/SSO sign-in
- invalid: request was malformed

email.description: Account email; only that user may approve the code in the dashboard.

mpc_custody.description: MPC custody mode to enable at creation (e.g. "2-of-2", "2-of-3")

cmek_fingerprint.description: SHA-256 fingerprint of the CMEK key (64 hex chars)

mpc_custody.description: MPC custody mode (e.g. "2-of-2", "2-of-3"), absent when MPC is not enabled

mpc_threshold.description: Number of shares required to reconstruct the key

fingerprint.description: SHA-256 hex fingerprint of the CMEK key (64 chars)

new_fingerprint.description: SHA-256 hex fingerprint of the new CMEK key

type.description: Secret type (generic, password, api_key, certificate, private_key, ssh_key, env)

is_disabled.description: Whether this version has been disabled (retained for audit but unreadable)

SecretCreatedResponse.description: Returned when a secret is created or updated. Extends SecretMetadataResponse with an optional client_share for MPC vaults.

client_share.description: Base64-encoded client key share. Returned only for MPC 2-of-2 vaults. The client must store this share securely — it is not persisted server-side.

cmek_encrypted.description: Whether this secret value is CMEK-encrypted (requires client-side decryption)

charset.description: Character set for the generated value (default hex)

type.description: Override the secret type (defaults to existing secret's type)

name.description: Display name for the new agent

human_email.description:
Optional. If set, pending enrollment is bound to that 1Claw account email
and Allow/Deny links are emailed. If omitted, only `approval_url` is used
(link-only enrollment; human must open the URL while signed in).

agent_id.description: UUID of the created agent (nil UUID until approved — uniform response)

message.description: Status message (worded to limit email enumeration where applicable)

approval_url.description:
Present when a pending enrollment was created and the client should show
this link (email flow includes it as a fallback; name-only flow requires it).

auth_method.description: Authentication method. api_key generates a one-time key; mtls requires client_cert_fingerprint; oidc_client_credentials requires oidc_issuer and oidc_client_id.

token_ttl_seconds.description: Per-agent token TTL in seconds (overrides global default)

vault_ids.description: Restrict agent to specific vault UUIDs (empty = all vaults in org)

client_cert_fingerprint.description: SHA-256 fingerprint of the client certificate (required for mTLS auth)

oidc_issuer.description: OIDC issuer URL (required for oidc_client_credentials auth)

oidc_client_id.description: OIDC client ID (required for oidc_client_credentials auth)

shroud_enabled.description: Enable Shroud LLM Proxy for this agent

federation_enabled.description:
Enable OIDC federation (RFC 8693 token-exchange) for this agent.
When true, the agent may call POST /v1/auth/federated-token to mint
federation tokens for the audiences listed in `federation_audiences`.

federation_audiences.description:
Allowlist of `aud` values the federation token-exchange may issue
tokens for (e.g. `["https://api.anthropic.com"]`). Empty array
blocks all federation requests (zero-trust default).

federated_token_ttl_seconds.description:
Per-agent TTL override for federation tokens (seconds). NULL falls
back to the global default (`ONECLAW_JWT_FEDERATED_TOKEN_EXPIRY_SECS`).
Hard-capped at 3600 seconds.

tx_spent_today_eth.description: ETH value already spent today (UTC) from recorded transactions; used with daily limit guardrails

client_cert_fingerprint.description: SHA-256 fingerprint of the client certificate (mTLS agents)

ssh_public_key.description: Ed25519 SSH public key (base64-encoded, auto-generated at creation)

ecdh_public_key.description: P-256 ECDH public key (base64 SEC1 uncompressed point, auto-generated at creation)

shroud_enabled.description: Whether this agent routes LLM traffic through the Shroud TEE proxy

federation_enabled.description:
Whether this agent may mint OIDC federation tokens via
POST /v1/auth/federated-token. False by default.

federation_audiences.description: Allowlist of audience URIs the agent may federate to.

federated_token_ttl_seconds.description: Per-agent TTL override for federation tokens (60..=3600).

signing_chains.description: Chains for which this agent has provisioned signing keys.

eip712_domain_allowlist.description: JSON array of allowed EIP-712 domain entries.

eip712_default_policy.description: Default EIP-712 policy (deny blocks all unless allowlisted).

AgentSmartAccountResponse.description: One Safe smart account per chain for an agent

ecdh_public_key.description: P-256 ECDH public key (base64 SEC1 uncompressed point)

shroud_enabled.description: Whether this agent routes LLM traffic through the Shroud TEE proxy

injection_threshold.description: Prompt injection score threshold (0.0–1.0). Requests above are blocked

allowed_providers.description: LLM providers this agent may use (empty = all)

daily_budget_usd.description: Daily LLM spend cap in USD (0 = unlimited)

enable_secret_redaction.description: Whether vault secrets are redacted from prompts/responses

enable_response_filtering.description: Whether response credential scanning is active

sanitization_mode.description: Global behavior when threats are detected (block=reject, surgical=remove only malicious parts, log_only=audit without action)

threat_logging.description: Whether to log all detected threats to audit (even when action is allow/warn)

flagged_request_retention_days.description: Number of days to retain flagged request bodies for replay/investigation

UnicodeNormalizationConfig.description: Unicode normalization and homoglyph detection settings

strip_zero_width.description: Remove zero-width and invisible Unicode characters

normalize_homoglyphs.description: Replace look-alike characters (e.g., Cyrillic а → Latin a)

action.description: Action when command injection is detected

custom_patterns.description: Custom regex patterns for detection (only used when patterns=custom)

SocialEngineeringConfig.description: Social engineering and manipulation detection settings

action.description: Action when manipulation attempts are detected
Action when obfuscated content is detected
Action when suspicious URLs are detected

blocked_domains.description: Domains to always block (e.g., pastebin.com, ngrok.io)

allowed_domains.description: Domains to always allow (allowlist mode when non-empty)

enabled.description: Enable filesystem path detection (disabled by default as it can be noisy)

action.description: Action when filesystem paths are detected

scan_arguments.description: Scan tool call arguments for credential exfiltration

block_credential_exfil.description: Block tool calls that appear to exfiltrate credentials

action.description: Action when a tool call violation is detected

OutputPolicy.description: Output content policy settings for LLM responses

blocked_patterns.description: Custom regex patterns to block in responses

blocked_entities.description: Named entities to block (e.g., competitor names)

block_harmful_content.description: Block responses containing harmful content categories

action.description: Action when output policy is violated

SecretInjectionConfig.description: Detects credentials injected into prompts that are not from the vault

action.description: Action when injected credentials are detected

AdvancedRedactionConfig.description: Advanced secret redaction settings (base64-encoded, split, prefix leaks)

detect_split_secrets.description: Detect secrets split across tokens or messages

detect_prefix_leak.description: Detect partial/prefix leaks of vault secrets

min_secret_length.description: Minimum secret length to consider for advanced matching

allowed_topics.description: Topics the agent is allowed to discuss (empty = all)

allowed_tasks.description: Tasks the agent is allowed to perform (empty = all)

action.description: Action when semantic policy is violated

api_key.description: One-time API key (only present for api_key auth method)

signing_key_path.description: Vault path to signing key. Auto-resolves per-chain signing key if provisioned, otherwise keys/{chain}-signer.
Vault path to signing key. Auto-resolves per-chain signing key if provisioned, otherwise keys/{chain}-signer.
Vault path to signing key. Auto-resolves per-chain signing key if provisioned, otherwise keys/{chain}-signer.
Vault path to signing key. Auto-resolves per-chain signing key if provisioned, otherwise keys/{chain}-signer.

signed_tx.description: Raw signed transaction hex. On GET list and GET by id, this property is omitted by default (absent from the response). Pass `include_signed_tx=true` on those endpoints to include it. Always present on the initial POST submit response.

LlmMeteredInvoiceLine.description: Metered line from Stripe upcoming invoice (usage detail when available).

quantity.description: Billed usage units when Stripe returns quantity (e.g. tokens).

LlmBillingCycleUsage.description: Accrued LLM charges for the current Stripe subscription period (upcoming invoice).

duration_days.description: How many days the tier lasts (default 365). Use 90 for a 3-month trial.

chains.description: Chains to generate wallets for (e.g. ["ethereum", "solana"]). Omit for all supported chains.

private_key_hex.description: Raw private key in hex encoding. Handle with extreme care.

response_injection_categories.description: Category tags emitted by the response-side filters (e.g. `markdown_image_exfil`, `data_uri_blob`, `echoed_instruction`).

external_urls_flagged.description: URLs emitted by the model that were flagged as potential exfil / callback targets.

unexpected_code_blocks.description: Number of code fences in the response that were not expected for the agent's allowed task set.

content_filtered.description: True when Shroud rewrote or blocked response content before returning it to the agent.

oidc_audience.description: Expected audience claim for OIDC token validation
Expected audience claim for OIDC token validation
Expected audience claim for OIDC token validation

api_key.description: The platform API key. Save immediately - it cannot be retrieved again.

spec.description:
Template specification defining vault, agents, policies, and signing keys to bootstrap.
Top-level fields: `vault` (object with name, description), `agents` (array of agent specs
with name, description, shroud_enabled, intents, shroud_config), `policies` (array with
vault_ref, principal_ref, paths, permissions, conditions), and `signing_keys` (array of
`{ chain }` objects — supported chains: ethereum, bitcoin, solana, xrp, cardano, tron).
When `signing_keys` is present, HSM-backed signing keys are auto-provisioned for the
bootstrapped agent.

subject_token.description: OIDC JWT from the platform's IdP (verified against JWKS)

email.description: Fallback when subject_token is not provided

template_id.description: Template to use. Falls back to the app's default template.

return_to.description: URL to redirect the user to after claiming resources.

expires_in.description: Seconds until the claim token expires

agent_api_key.description: One-time agent API key (ocv_ prefix). Store securely — not retrievable later.

signing_keys.description: Provisioned signing key details (chain, address, public key)

public_key_pem.description: PEM-encoded public key for step-up challenge signing

attestation_blob.description: Optional platform attestation (Apple DeviceCheck / Android SafetyNet)

target_id.description: ID of the resource the action targets

action_bound_hash.description: SHA-256 binding the challenge to the requested action and target

signature.description: Signature over the challenge nonce using the device's private key

step_up_token.description: Short-lived token authorizing the bound action

token.description: Push notification token from APNs or FCM

reason.description: Optional human-readable reason for the decision

summary.description: Structured summary of the action requiring approval
